channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
item.description: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.
item.content:encoded:
The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth scolds NATO allies — again
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth blasts NATO allies as a "paper tiger" while launching a six-month review of U.S. troop deployments in Europe and pressing for Europe to take the lead on its own defense.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: U.S. lifts blockade on Iranian ports as 60-day clock for a final deal starts ticking
item.description: The U.S. is allowing ships to enter and exit Iranian ports and coastal areas as the countries move to a new phase of negotiations over the next 60 days.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is allowing ships to enter and exit Iranian ports and coastal areas as the countries move to a new phase of negotiations over the next 60 days.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: NPR Politics and Policy Desk

item.title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
item.description: Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.
item.content:encoded:
Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.

(Image credit: Nathan Laine)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
item.description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Report: Russia's nuclear-powered 'Skyfall' missile is dirty and dangerous
item.description: MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."
item.content:encoded:
MIT researchers think they've worked out exactly how Russia's Burevestnik nuclear-powered missile flies. "It's almost certainly a terrible idea," one analyst said. "But it's not an impossible idea."

(Image credit: Russian Ministry of Defense)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Trump signs agreement with Iran. And, the president's approval hits record lows
item.description: Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.
item.content:encoded:
Trump signed a preliminary agreement with Iran yesterday to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. And, the president's approval rating has hit a record low, according to a new NPR poll.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Hegseth announces in Brussels a review of U.S. forces in Europe, and a 'NATO 3.0'
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."

(Image credit: Virginia Mayo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: For Lebanon, a U.S.-Iran agreement raises questions beyond Hezbollah
item.description: Former Lebanese ambassador to Jordan, Tracy Chamoun, explains what the U.S.-Iran agreement means for Lebanon — and what a ceasefire would look like on the ground.
item.content:encoded:
Former Lebanese ambassador to Jordan, Tracy Chamoun, explains what the U.S.-Iran agreement means for Lebanon — and what a ceasefire would look like on the ground.

(Image credit: Jack Guez)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: FIFA hydration breaks have sparked criticism. But what do they actually do?
item.description: For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
